name: Password managers
summary: Consumer password managers (1Password, Bitwarden, Dashlane, LastPass, KeePass, etc.), free-$60/year.

evidence.claim: NIST SP 800-63B Digital Identity Guidelines recommend password managers and strong, unique passwords per service. Guidance updated 2023-revision.
LastPass 2022 data breach: attackers exfiltrated encrypted customer vault backups; incident response disclosed December 2022, post-incident analysis ongoing into 2024.
CISA 2024 Cybersecurity Best Practices guidance explicitly recommending password managers with on-device encryption, zero-knowledge architecture, and regular third-party audits.
SOC 2 Type 2 compliance is the common third-party attestation for cloud-based password managers; SOC 2 + annual penetration test is the minimum enterprise-grade bar.

criteria.name: zero_knowledge_architecture
criteria.notes: Vendor cannot decrypt your vault (E3). Table-stakes for serious password managers.

criteria.name: open_source
criteria.notes: Independent code audit possible; higher trust for security-critical tool.

criteria.name: third_party_audit_recent
criteria.notes: Annual penetration test by reputable firm (Cure53, Trail of Bits, NCC Group) (E4).

criteria.name: breach_disclosed_last_3yr
criteria.notes: LastPass 2022 is the canonical cautionary example (E2).

criteria.name: passkey_support
criteria.notes: FIDO2 passkeys are the post-password future.

criteria.name: emergency_access_or_recovery_option
criteria.notes: Without a recovery mechanism, losing the master password = losing the vault.

confabulationPatterns.reality: AES-256 is the industry standard; 'bank-grade' is marketing. What matters is key-derivation (PBKDF2 with ≥600,000 iterations or Argon2id, per 2023 OWASP) (E1) and whether the vendor can decrypt your vault.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: What KDF is used (PBKDF2 or Argon2id), and with how many iterations? Is the architecture zero-knowledge?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Meaningless claim. Specific questions: does the vendor publish a security whitepaper, has it had a third-party audit in the last 12 months (E4), and has it disclosed a breach.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Has the vendor published a recent third-party audit and security whitepaper?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Claim should be verified against the vendor's own security bulletin page; absence of disclosure isn't proof of no breach. Check HIBP and vendor-specific post-mortems.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Does the vendor publish a security-incident page, and what does it list?

counterfeitSignals.signal: Free password-manager app from unknown publisher on app store
counterfeitSignals.action: flag — password-manager malware disguised as legitimate apps is a growing attack vector

compatibilityQuestions: How many devices/platforms? Free Bitwarden covers unlimited devices; some competitors limit free tier.
Family use? Most paid plans include 5-6 seats; check sharing-vault UX.
Cloud vs self-hosted preference? KeePass is local-only; Bitwarden supports self-hosted server.

representativeSkus.name: Bitwarden Free
representativeSkus.notes: Open-source reference point for the category; passes every criterion.

representativeSkus.name: 1Password Individual
representativeSkus.notes: Closed-source but most rigorous annual audit cadence; Secret Key adds second factor to master password.

representativeSkus.name: LastPass Premium
representativeSkus.notes: 2022 vault-backup breach (E2); post-incident rebuilding trust. Formerly category leader; now the cautionary data point.

representativeSkus.name: KeePassXC
representativeSkus.notes: Local-only, no cloud sync. Maximum privacy; user manages sync + backup manually.
